rawtext.text:
Lower your voice. Some of my stock is sleeping.

Sella Nightlantern. I deal in relics: things dug out of barrows, fished from drowned churches, or won at card tables from people who should never have been holding them. I walk the long circuit — six days, six villages — and I only sell what I'd trust at my own back.

You're the Awoken. Yes, obviously. My lantern has been leaning toward you since the gate.

Memory. It burns memory — mine, donated a candle-hour at a time. Blue light shows me what things §oare§r, not what they claim to be. A crown that swears it cannot rust. A blade that says it's only ever been decorative. The lantern coughs at liars.

I bought the trick from a man at the Cinderford crossroads, years ago, for one summer afternoon I honestly don't miss. Only later did I learn what else that man was buying, and from whom, and I have been walking the circuit ahead of him ever since.

He is buying up the hills' memories, Awoken. When a village forgets why it fears the dark, it stops barring the door. §dSpend your regard here carefully — in Elderfall, being remembered fondly is armor.§r

§6Nightlantern's Errand§r — the lantern runs low, and I decant its light into glass. Bring me §f6 glass bottles§r and §f4 glowstone dust§r.

In exchange: Soulleech. A blade with a thirst, won regrettably. Better it drinks for Elderfall than against it.

Touch nothing that hisses. Prices in emeralds:

§bTome: Frostbind§r — freeze your enemies where they stand — §a30§r
§7Tome: Stoneskin§r — skin like the old ruins — §a35§r
§9Tome: Skystrike§r — lightning where you look — §a45§r
§8Voidrend§r — a blade that withers what it cuts — §a48§r
§5Runeplate of the Awoken§r — deepens and quickens your mana — §a60§r
